Being halfway through the quarter is a natural opportunity for us to check in internally on our progress. And if we continue on our current trajectory, we will not reach our carefully-calculated fundraising goal.

While the sky won’t literally fall if we don’t hit our goal, we will be significantly disadvantaged in our competitiveness for 2024. (Really.) I owe it to my supporters to be real – and to do everything I can to put us in the best position to hold this seat we worked so hard to win.

name: Team Lucas
subject: The status quo is afraid
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
...and they should be
#
Peter,
The status quo is afraid of Lucas Kunce — and they should be.
**FIRST —** A poll showed Josh Hawley underwater with Missouri voters, with _“39 percent holding**strongly unfavorable views**_ of him compared to just 26 percent who are strongly favorable."
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/33005?refcode=em20230512-3112&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=3112%2E614464%2EUhozyk)
**THEN —** Our campaign outraised Hawley by nearly 2-to-1.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/33005?refcode=em20230512-3112&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3112%2E614464%2EUhozyk)
**AND NOW —** The GOP’s favorite oppo shop has launched EVEN MORE desperate attacks at Lucas for... playing Magic: the Gathering.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/33005?refcode=em20230512-3112&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=3112%2E614464%2EUhozyk)
**They know Lucas Kunce has what it takes to defeat Josh Hawley and flip Missouri’s Senate seat** — so now, they’re doing, saying, and spending anything they can to tear him down before it’s too late.
We need your help to fight back, Peter. So we’re asking:
**[Will you rush a donation of $10 or whatever you can toward our grassroots campaign to elect Lucas? Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. **** _Republicans Fail Seniors with Lack of Cost-of-Living Adjustment or 13th Check_ For the past three decades, the state legislature has provided Hoosier public service retirees a cost-of-living adjustment or 13th check to help them keep up with rising costs. This year, however, Republicans failed seniors and [didn’t include](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200711/405939585/-504675127?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) such a proposal in the state budget. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200712/405939586/-435389620?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) [Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200713/405939587/-435389620?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) With the cost of living up, and Hoosier seniors struggling with inflation, this year was as important as any to include extra help for those on a fixed income. Democrats fought for Hoosier seniors and will continue fighting to make sure they get the raise they need to pay for essentials such as food, housing, and medicine. **** _Diego Morales Goes to Hungary Hours After Municipal Primary Elections_ Within 48 hours of the last ballots being cast in Indiana’s municipal primaries, and with military ballots still arriving throughout the state, Secretary of State Diego Morales [headed to Hungary](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200714/405939588/1377562245?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) to hang out with a dictator and fellow election deniers like Kari Lake. [__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200715/405939589/-1126409197?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535)[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200716/405939590/-1126409197?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) Hoosiers deserve elected officials who show up and do their job at critical times. From the start of his term this January, Diego has been a walking controversy. From [hiring his brother-in-law](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200717/405939591/-1105328856?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) to promoting laws that [restrict the right to vote](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200718/405939592/-1530374468?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535), he has shown he cannot be trusted. [Read INDems release](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200719/405939593/-1765597967?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535). ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200720/405939594/-2108900706?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) [Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200721/405939595/-2043469694?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) State Rep. This year, she passed [legislation](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63200722/405939596/1987255991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNzE0NDc1Zi1lZWYwLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2R7KyT7Lp0nyEicglqpPE4P9_khrB6ol_-M5Miuy30A=&emci=bf20bac2-e3f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=3714475f-eef0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) to require the testing of water supplies in daycares and preschools for lead. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ** Republicans Fail Seniors with Lack of Cost-of-Living Adjustment or 13th Check For the past three decades, the state legislature has provided Hoosier public service retirees a cost-of-living adjustment or 13th check to help them keep up with rising costs. This year, however, Republicans failed seniors and such a proposal in the state budget. With the cost of living up, and Hoosier seniors struggling with inflation, this year was as important as any to include extra help for those on a fixed income. Democrats fought for Hoosier seniors and will continue fighting to make sure they get the raise they need to pay for essentials such as food, housing, and medicine. Diego Morales Goes to Hungary Hours After Municipal Primary Elections Within 48 hours of the last ballots being cast in Indiana’s municipal primaries, and with military ballots still arriving throughout the state, Secretary of State Diego Morales to hang out with a dictator and fellow election deniers like Kari Lake. Hoosiers deserve elected officials who show up and do their job at critical times. From the start of his term this January, Diego has been a walking controversy. From to promoting laws that , he has shown he cannot be trusted. . Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. State Rep. Carolyn Jackson (Hammond) has been fighting for years to ensure drinking water is safe for Hoosier students. This year, she passed to require the testing of water supplies in daycares and preschools for lead. This follows her success in 2020 to mandate testing of drinking water in schools. The bill was recently signed into law by the Governor. Upcoming Candidate Trainings Saturday, May 13, 12-5 p.m. ET: “Train-the-Trainer (T3) Summit”: This training summit is only held once a year by the NDTC. Learn skills to take back to your local parties, campaigns, and volunteers. Register by . Tuesday, May 16, 1-2 p.m. ET: “How to Maximize Call Time and Raise More Money”: Learn more about raising money for your campaign and outreach efforts. Get important advice on developing a hard fundraising ask. Register by . Rural Urban Bridge Initiative Virtual Training Tuesday, May 23, 7-8 p.m. ET: RUBI’s presentation explains how the rural-urban divide came to be and suggests what progressive groups, candidates, and parties can do about it. The objective is to defuse hostility and contempt, find mutual understanding and common ground, and build multi-racial working and middle-class rural solidarity between rural, suburban, and urban dwellers. Our practical solutions are based on research by our own and other scholars as well as the personal experiences of rural progressive organizers. Register by ! name: Freedom Nation Today
subject: Access “C.S. Lewis on Christianity”
body:
C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions

| This sponsored message from Freedom Nation Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=09146f56859a49629f587cabb7f3345e&_e=oLAaqcFf41RzfvBTp50NHsm7JzA6cKhSunaqN5o1ri78cC1wF4_wI0L9jIASRZvpEkSXRJCqhPRglHB4hvpeBfxHqR7u0RMjbHtGTlpZc3J460jZZQ1liGcZTKIwPit1AA4-E_D5duGV3ZATPp7zwnXO1HI_faPP_rQs4Ybk3VGvsz_eNVhvYnMpimVOLv_b7DYsYYz4aihr17T7fOayixr78zj-LGF026w__RvZRnFbSu_ZbCpMx18Ar5mhkHttkBOBzCi8BOXW8_qitEmzeRSmb1mPY6foAWVT2JMUXlNrPtx9RkDX1IT6UiqiW3JLS0TzBufTBFl1gOBAP0y5a866QUwSONo0OTEBt4WwhKGm9lMT2zfLwskRk1MVX5SQE5tP8ku7dzkzkGulzVhxxlKAC20Axeoo9HSyrtMQzk0KVMqnMrMvXxcOxuIF5WB1)
---
| | Dear friend, C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics to become believers and even more believers to renew and deepen their faith--making him the greatest Christian apologist of the twentieth century.

That's why we produced "[**C.S. Lewis on Christianity,**](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=09146f56859a49629f587cabb7f3345e&_e=oLAaqcFf41RzfvBTp50NHsm7JzA6cKhSunaqN5o1ri78cC1wF4_wI0L9jIASRZvpEkSXRJCqhPRglHB4hvpeBfxHqR7u0RMjbHtGTlpZc3J460jZZQ1liGcZTKIwPit1AA4-E_D5duGV3ZATPp7zwnXO1HI_faPP_rQs4Ybk3VGvsz_eNVhvYnMpimVOLv_b7DYsYYz4aihr17T7fOayixr78zj-LGF026w__RvZRnFbSu_ZbCpMx18Ar5mhkHttkBOBzCi8BOXW8_qitEmzeQJDFSetg0uIOwjBEHGUT_UKUc1hUsX-NOzTIA3KD5R0KMTeAOLxOsTHUeP0gGdrQeLWk61AIe6urXs2L6o7SZ22a_29fxzLdQxVCvTiztqPgyI9j77HKK5BvYJnlC0E7MVTMA-SgJRRgNLDMT162tqpYTPn3UWrvt418CQNU-79)" which you can now own on DVD.
---
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=09146f56859a49629f587cabb7f3345e&_e=oLAaqcFf41RzfvBTp50NHsm7JzA6cKhSunaqN5o1ri78cC1wF4_wI0L9jIASRZvpEkSXRJCqhPRglHB4hvpeBfxHqR7u0RMjbHtGTlpZc3J460jZZQ1liGcZTKIwPit1AA4-E_D5duGV3ZATPp7zwnXO1HI_faPP_rQs4Ybk3VGvsz_eNVhvYnMpimVOLv_b7DYsYYz4aihr17T7fOayixr78zj-LGF026w__RvZRnFbSu_ZbCpMx18Ar5mhkHttkBOBzCi8BOXW8_qitEmzefk7CHMCu-wHU7YaK5fgDMIbC7Y7kSRs2Y1-irqDEyrhgiXmmDupjaDFGNcPxU8h6K4kdTfA07iyg-Dxxg04tHYHkJyDV8WS36ptk7_hPqPmJelG9pt3qX-3TcTbYNuxP42WG0kbw2bEg9SrE8nRRkxONdg22_JgiOZdsN1oHG-p)
---
| | In this course you will learn Lewis's profound insights on the central topics of Christianity, including:
* good and evil,
* conversion and faith,
* prayer and the Bible,
* suffering and death,
* heaven and hell, and much more.

Dr. Ward is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Hillsdale College and a member of the Faculty of Theology and Religion at the University of Oxford. He is the author of _Planet Narnia: The Seven Heavens in the Imagination of C.S. Lewis_ and _After Humanity: A Guide to C.S. Lewis 's The Abolition of Man_, and the co-editor of _The Cambridge Companion to C.S. Lewis_. He even spent three years living in Lewis's Oxford home as the resident Warden of The Kilns!

This course is especially moving because it examines C.S. Lewis's beautiful writings about faith and traces Lewis's own journey from skeptic to believer. The combination provides an opportunity for us to grow in our knowledge and understanding of Christianity.

That's why I hope you'll join us in examining the eternal questions of Christianity with C.S. Lewis with [this beautiful new DVD box set](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=09146f56859a49629f587cabb7f3345e&_e=oLAaqcFf41RzfvBTp50NHsm7JzA6cKhSunaqN5o1ri78cC1wF4_wI0L9jIASRZvpEkSXRJCqhPRglHB4hvpeBfxHqR7u0RMjbHtGTlpZc3J460jZZQ1liGcZTKIwPit1AA4-E_D5duGV3ZATPp7zwnXO1HI_faPP_rQs4Ybk3VGvsz_eNVhvYnMpimVOLv_b7DYsYYz4aihr17T7fOayixr78zj-LGF026w__RvZRnFbSu_ZbCpMx18Ar5mhkHttkBOBzCi8BOXW8_qitEmzeaHKbxMdf2Zz1oeGKBo65CvEZ9FUn5G5r776Qq0FN2OSUjiVCwbxlj8c1tQj3Cu94M9qKmRDWsjkyNsWJ9JNKkCcpH7_vof-LPVGaF4eCQ5eooTZ36u-_b1_JjaIivOuOSZFEc22xWhUhByxfqwYU1EJvvBOSnqy7CxFdp_XEqpG).

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

name: The New Pennsylvania Project
subject: Are you ready to make your voice heard on Tuesday, May 16?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project
body:
https://secure.everyaction.com/DQ7Hxcpq3Eabel6LA-9V0Q2?refcode=newsletter [https://secure.everyaction.com/DQ7Hxcpq3Eabel6LA-9V0Q2?refcode=newsletter]
Good afternoon, friends, advocates and voters.
We hope this special edition of the New Pennsylvania Project eBlast finds you well.
Tuesday, May 16 is the Primary Election in Pennsylvania and we want to make sure you have all the necessary information to make your voice heard!
From judges to county leaders to school boards, the stakes are high in 2023. All of these positions are on the ballot and will impact our rights and freedom. There are no off-year elections in Pennsylvania.
What is a Primary Election?
In The Primary Election, each political party selects its candidates to run for office during the general election. The candidates who get the highest number of votes in the primary election go on to run in the general election. Primary Elections allow voters to get to know the candidates that will be participating in the General Elections, IF they have winning votes during the primary election first.
Primary elections determine which candidate will represent each political party before the General Election. Primary elections give the voter the opportunity to decide who , from a pool of candidates, should be nominated by their political party to run in the General Election.
Closed Primaries: Voters in PA must be registered party members in order to vote in that party’s primaries. Many new voters may register outside a major party but be unaware that prevents them from participating in the primary.
Special Elections in Delaware and Montour/Northumberland Counties
There are two Special Elections happening on May 16 in Delaware County (HD 163) and Montour/Northumberland Counties (HD 108). These special elections are open to ALL voters who live in PA House District 163 and PA House District 108.
These special elections are partisan elections and will determine who represents those districts within the Pennsylvania State House.
PA House District 163 includes: Aldan, Clifton Heights, Collingdale, Darby Township (Ward 3, 4, and 5), and parts of Upper Darby Township to include districts 1, 2, 3 and 5.
PA House District includes the entirety of Montour County and parts of Northumberland County some including: Delaware Township, East Chillisquaque Township, and Lewis Township.

Voting on Election Day
The polls are open from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. If it is 8 p.m. and you are still in line, stay in line. You are permitted to vote as long as you are in line by 8 p.m.
Voters should check their polling place as locations may have changed: https://www.vote.org/polling-place-locator/
If you are a first time voter or voting at a new polling location, you will need to bring a form of ID.*Acceptable ID: driver’s license, U.S. passport, voter registration card, military, student or employee ID, firearm permit, current utility bill or bank statement, paycheck or government check and any ID issued by PA or federal government.
If you did not return your mail-in or absentee ballot and you want to vote in person, you have two options:
Bring your ballot and the pre-addressed outer return envelope to your polling place to be voided. After you surrender your ballot and envelope and sign a declaration, you can then vote a regular ballot.
If you don't surrender your ballot and return envelope, you can only vote by provisional ballot at your polling place. Your county election board will then verify that you did not vote by mail before counting your provisional ballot.
Voting By Mail
The deadline to request a mail in ballot was May 9th. If you have requested a mail in ballot and have received that ballot, read the instructions and make your candidate selections carefully. Make sure to vote in every race on the ballot.
Pack and seal it
Put your ballot in the smaller secrecy envelope and seal it. This is the envelope that only says “Official Election Ballot.”
Put the smaller secrecy envelope in the larger ballot-return envelope
This is the envelope with space for your address on the front and the Voter’s Declaration
Sign it, date it, seal it
Complete the entire “Voter’s Declaration” on the ballot-return envelope. Make sure to sign and date it
Seal the ballot-return envelope
Return it by mail or dropbox
By mail— Add a stamp if needed and drop it in the mail
USPS will still deliver ballots with missing or inadequate postage
In person—Drop off at your County Board of Elections, a satellite voting location or at a secure drop box
You can designate someone to drop off your ballot if you have a disability
One designated agent can return up to 2 ballots, theirs and someone else’s. You will need to fill out this form (https://www.vote.pa.gov/Resources/Documents/Authorize-Designated-Agent-for-Mail-in-or-Absentee-Ballot.pdf?emci=0469b664-14f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=c4f57bfe-17f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=5480605) and contact your county election office for information about how and where to return the completed form.
Early Voting
Cast your mail-in or absentee ballot all in one visit to your county election board or other officially designated site: https://www.vote.pa.gov/Resources/Pages/Contact-Your-Election-Officials.aspx
With this option, there is no need for mail at all, and you can cast your vote at your convenience.
Learn more about voting early in-person by mail-in or absentee ballot: https://www.vote.pa.gov/Voting-in-PA/Pages/Early-Voting.aspx

Commonly Asked Questions
Q: Do I need an ID to vote in person on Election Day?
A: NO, unless you are a first-time voter, or if you are voting in a new precinct.
*Acceptable ID: driver’s license, U.S. passport, voter registration card, military, student or employee ID, firearm permit, current utility bill or bank statement, paycheck or government check and any ID issued by PA or federal government.
Q: Can someone get assistance at their polling place if they do not speak English?
A: There are currently three counties in Pennsylvania that should provide voting materials and assistance in Spanish:
Berks County
Lehigh County
Philadelphia County
*Philadelphia County is currently the only county that also can provide voting materials and assistance in Chinese

Q: What if I want to vote in person but already requested a mail in or absentee ballot?
A: If you did not return your mail-in or absentee ballot and you want to vote in person, you have two options:
Bring your ballot and the pre-addressed outer return envelope to your polling place to be voided. After you surrender your ballot and envelope and sign a declaration, you can then vote a regular ballot.
If you don't surrender your ballot and return envelope, you can only vote by provisional ballot at your polling place. Your county election board will then verify that you did not vote by mail before counting your provisional ballot.
Q: Can I designate someone to drop off my ballot if I am disabled?
A: Yes, you can designate someone to drop off your ballot if you have a disability.
One designated agent can return up to 2 ballots, theirs and someone else’s.
You will need to fill out this form (https://www.vote.pa.gov/Resources/Documents/Authorize-Designated-Agent-for-Mail-in-or-Absentee-Ballot.pdf?emci=0469b664-14f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=c4f57bfe-17f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=5480605) and contact your county election office for information about how and where to return the completed form.

Resources
PA Department of State Website: vote.pa.gov [http://www.vote.pa.gov]
Return Your Ballot: vote.pa/return [http://vote.pa/return]
Early Voting: vote.pa/early [http://vote.pa/early]
Track Your Ballot: vote.pa/track [http://www.vote.pa/track]
Find Your Polling Place: vote.pa/polls [http://vote.pa/polls]
Keystone Votes - Know Your Rights: keystonevotes.org/know-your-rights [https://www.keystonevotes.org/know-your-rights]
Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE
DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA
Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA
Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE
Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US
Thank you for taking the time to stay informed. We'll be back in two weeks with the latest edition.
In solidarity,
#TeamNPP

clean_body: [] Good afternoon, friends, advocates and voters. We hope this special edition of the New Pennsylvania Project eBlast finds you well. Tuesday, May 16 is the Primary Election in Pennsylvania and we want to make sure you have all the necessary information to make your voice heard! From judges to county leaders to school boards, the stakes are high in 2023. All of these positions are on the ballot and will impact our rights and freedom. There are no off-year elections in Pennsylvania. What is a Primary Election? In The Primary Election, each political party selects its candidates to run for office during the general election. The candidates who get the highest number of votes in the primary election go on to run in the general election. Primary Elections allow voters to get to know the candidates that will be participating in the General Elections, IF they have winning votes during the primary election first. Primary elections determine which candidate will represent each political party before the General Election. Primary elections give the voter the opportunity to decide who , from a pool of candidates, should be nominated by their political party to run in the General Election. Closed Primaries: Voters in PA must be registered party members in order to vote in that party’s primaries. Many new voters may register outside a major party but be unaware that prevents them from participating in the primary. Special Elections in Delaware and Montour/Northumberland Counties There are two Special Elections happening on May 16 in Delaware County (HD 163) and Montour/Northumberland Counties (HD 108). These special elections are open to ALL voters who live in PA House District 163 and PA House District 108. These special elections are partisan elections and will determine who represents those districts within the Pennsylvania State House. PA House District 163 includes: Aldan, Clifton Heights, Collingdale, Darby Township (Ward 3, 4, and 5), and parts of Upper Darby Township to include districts 1, 2, 3 and 5. PA House District includes the entirety of Montour County and parts of Northumberland County some including: Delaware Township, East Chillisquaque Township, and Lewis Township. Voting on Election Day The polls are open from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. If it is 8 p.m. and you are still in line, stay in line. You are permitted to vote as long as you are in line by 8 p.m. Voters should check their polling place as locations may have changed: If you are a first time voter or voting at a new polling location, you will need to bring a form of ID.Acceptable ID: driver’s license, U.S. passport, voter registration card, military, student or employee ID, firearm permit, current utility bill or bank statement, paycheck or government check and any ID issued by PA or federal government. If you did not return your mail-in or absentee ballot and you want to vote in person, you have two options: Bring your ballot and the pre-addressed outer return envelope to your polling place to be voided. After you surrender your ballot and envelope and sign a declaration, you can then vote a regular ballot. If you don't surrender your ballot and return envelope, you can only vote by provisional ballot at your polling place. Your county election board will then verify that you did not vote by mail before counting your provisional ballot. Voting By Mail The deadline to request a mail in ballot was May 9th. If you have requested a mail in ballot and have received that ballot, read the instructions and make your candidate selections carefully. Make sure to vote in every race on the ballot. Pack and seal it Put your ballot in the smaller secrecy envelope and seal it. This is the envelope that only says “Official Election Ballot.” Put the smaller secrecy envelope in the larger ballot-return envelope This is the envelope with space for your address on the front and the Voter’s Declaration Sign it, date it, seal it Complete the entire “Voter’s Declaration” on the ballot-return envelope. Make sure to sign and date it Seal the ballot-return envelope Return it by mail or dropbox By mail— Add a stamp if needed and drop it in the mail USPS will still deliver ballots with missing or inadequate postage In person—Drop off at your County Board of Elections, a satellite voting location or at a secure drop box You can designate someone to drop off your ballot if you have a disability One designated agent can return up to 2 ballots, theirs and someone else’s. You will need to fill out this form () and contact your county election office for information about how and where to return the completed form. subject: One Email. All the News. No mainstream media bias.
body:
Navigating your way to quality news online has become increasingly complicated—much like trying to put out a dumpster fire using a squirt-gun filled with gasoline.

| Please be honest...Who do you turn to for the Truth?

[ CORPORATE NEWS GIANTS ](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zl22jf15otp6u414kqdibssf7e82ehvn22nma6ng_rp22sh2s8i66o76cjh69pr4c9h2bs)

Navigating your way to quality news online has become increasingly complicated—much like trying to put out a dumpster fire using a squirt-gun filled with gasoline.

But you’re not alone.

What if there was a better way to get the news?

Sign up today for a **FREE** newsletter called [_The Flyover_](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zkb16majsqjecp6mgh1icfn2gscds36pu2eheb8g_rp22sh2s8i66o76cjh69pr4c9h2bs). name: Your Pending Renewal (via DLCC.org)
subject: re: Your Membership Card
body:
And while 16,000 members is still a great accomplishment, we’re still missing you on our membership list. | | |
---

name: Kim Bernice Nguyen
subject: We have to talk about mental health
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kim Nguyen for Congress
body:
| | May is Mental Health Awareness Month and I want to take this opportunity to talk about the mental health crisis in our country.

1 in 5 U.S. adults experiences mental illness, and while our mental health professionals are doing amazing work providing health care, we still have a long way to go in ensuring that everyone has the resources they need to take care of themselves.

It’s critical that we increase awareness about the mental health issues that millions of Americans are facing – _but House Republicans are trying to sweep mental health care under the rug as they attempt to cut funding for mental health services for veterans and students._

[Sign on now to condemn Republicans’ actions and join me in calling for increased access to mental health support for all.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63202777/405947243/-1461721375?refcode=KBN_EM_SV_90DO_230512_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM4MzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWJmOGQzNGQtZjJmMC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FhkL7ryvcMT8HVCu7PHS1ZTi8-3D58Ekc_mGbyPMIlU=&emci=8aa32b8d-61f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=1bf8d34d-f2f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9674971) | | **[SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63202778/405947271/-1461721375?refcode=KBN_EM_SV_90DO_230512_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM4MzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWJmOGQzNGQtZjJmMC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FhkL7ryvcMT8HVCu7PHS1ZTi8-3D58Ekc_mGbyPMIlU=&emci=8aa32b8d-61f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=1bf8d34d-f2f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9674971)**

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dave=20Min=20for=20Congress?=
subject: RELEASE: Police Officers Endorse Dave Min
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dave Min for Congress 2024**
body:
The Los Angeles Police Protective League, California’s Largest Local Law Enforcement Association Endorses Min | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/5fd0f77c0859/release-police-officers-endorse-dave-min-15655391?e=7017cc100e)
---
| |
---
| | **For Immediate Release:** May 12, 2023
**Contact:**[press@davemin.com](mailto:press@davemin.com)
**Police Officers Endorse Dave Min** _The Los Angeles Police Protective League, California’s Largest Local Law Enforcement Association, Endorses Min_ **IRVINE, CA** — Today, the Los Angeles Police Protective League (LAPPL), California’s largest local law enforcement association, representing over 9,200 police officers, and professionally sworn members of the LAPD, endorsed Dave Min for Congress in California’s 47th Congressional District.

**“State Senator Dave Min has been a leader in collaborating with law enforcement leaders across the state to stop policies that undermine the protection of local communities,”** said LAPPL President Craig Lally.**“He has supported fully funding public safety and providing officers the resources they need to serve and protect our communities. He has advocated for thoughtful, tough, and meaningful legislation to keep our neighborhoods safe. Police Officers stand with Dave Min and LAPPL is proud to endorse him for Congress.”** This early endorsement is just the latest in a long string of decisive endorsements for California State Senator Dave Min, who launched his campaign for CA-47 in January with the support of incumbent Congresswoman Katie Porter, and recently announced his campaign’s early endorsement by the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) and the California Federation of Teachers. Just over three months into his campaign, Senator Min has built a coalition of support that includes over 125 local, state, and federal officials, including California Lt. Governor Eleni Kounalakis, Attorney General Rob Bonta, and three other California statewide elected officials, AAPI Victory Fund, Asian American Action Fund, the Asian American and Pacific Islander Congressional Caucus’s ASPIRE PAC, which represents 21 members of Congress, and many others. A full endorsement list is available[ here](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=0a1d943712&e=7017cc100e). Senator Min, the only Korean-American in the state legislature, serves as Chair of the Senate Natural Resources and Water Committee and as Senate Vice Chair of the Asian and Pacific Islander Legislative Caucus. Min, who was formerly a law professor at UC Irvine School of Law and has three children in Irvine public schools, also serves on the Senate’s Budget Subcommittee for Education, which oversees all budget funding for California’s public schools, community colleges, the California State University system, and the University of California system. Min has proven himself an incredibly effective legislative leader in the Senate, authoring 16 bills signed into law by the Governor, including leading legislation on [hate crimes](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=4135aaab6c&e=7017cc100e), [gun violence prevention](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=991c798750&e=7017cc100e), family safety, and [women’s reproductive rights](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=95e306daaa&e=7017cc100e), and procuring over $70 million in direct appropriations for priorities in his district, including for fire response, parks and open spaces, and climate innovation. In the wake of the 2021 Huntington Beach Oil Spill, he stood up to the oil industry and led efforts to end [offshore drilling](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=718ecbc10a&e=7017cc100e). Min sits on two key environmental committees, chairing the Natural Resources and Water Committee and serving on the Energy and Utilities Committee, as well as the Budget Committee, the Budget Subcommittee for Education, the Judiciary Committee, and the Banking and Financial Institutions Committee. Senator Min previously served as Chair of the Select Committee on Cybersecurity. Before entering the State Senate, Min was a law professor at UC Irvine, where he taught and researched in the area of business law. He resides with his wife Jane Stoever, a UC Irvine law professor who specializes in domestic violence and family law, and their three children in Irvine. Dave is a native Californian, a product of California public schools who graduated from the Wharton School and Harvard Law School, and an Eagle Scout. ############ _California’s 47th is an open Congressional District in Orange County that includes the Cities of Costa Mesa, Huntington Beach, Irvine, Newport Beach, and Seal Beach, and portions of Laguna Beach, Laguna Hills, and Laguna Woods. Incumbent Katie Porter is vacating the seat to run for U.S. Senate. In 2022, Porter defeated former Assemblymember Republican Scott Baugh by 3.44% in this “toss-up” district. State Senator Dave Min currently represents over 80% of Congressional District 47 in Sacramento. In 2020, Min received over 158,000 votes within CA-47, defeating incumbent John Moorlach by 5.15% in this area. 19% of the registered voters in CA-47 are of Asian and Pacific Islander descent, including one of the largest Korean-American populations in the country. _
---
| |
---
| **Paid for by Dave Min for Congress 2024**
PO Box 5959
Irvine, CA 92616

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=205fb18a1d&e=7017cc100e) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://davemin.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1b97cb6a343b55cba640c3404&id=0638f99211&e=7017cc100e).

In 2020, Min received over 158,000 votes within CA-47, defeating incumbent John Moorlach by 5.15% in this area. 19% of the registered voters in CA-47 are of Asian and Pacific Islander descent, including one of the largest Korean-American populations in the country. | | | Paid for by Dave Min for Congress 2024 PO Box 5959 Irvine, CA 92616 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or . | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Dave Min For Senate PO Box 5959 Irvine, CA 92616-5959 * USA

name: HJ at United We Dream Action
subject: We deserve to live without fear of being separated from our families
body:
DACA ending means I could lose my job, be separated from my son, or forced to leave the country I call home. | | | Hi Peter, my name is HJ. I’m a UWDA member, a DACA recipient, and a mother of one. The first time I applied for DACA, I had just learned I was pregnant with my son, Alex. I wanted to protect him and provide a healthy environment for him — and DACA was the best way to do that while undocumented. DACA allowed me to provide for my son and live without the fear of arbitrary detention or deportation. **But even I know it’s not enough — for me and Alex, for hundreds of thousands of other DACA families, and millions more who don’t qualify for the program.** It’s terrifying to think that one rogue judge could shut down DACA at any moment. I might lose my job, be separated from my son, or even be forced to leave the country I call home. **Peter, that’s the harsh reality for many mothers like me as long as DACA recipients lack permanent protections — which is why United We Dream Action is leading the fight to secure a pathway to citizenship.** Alex and I will continue to show up to rallies in Washington, D.C. and around the country to meet with members of Congress and make our demands for permanent protections undeniable. We need you in this fight too, Peter. **Before Mother’s Day, will you make a donation to support the millions of mothers like me who are fighting for a pathway to citizenship? We deserve to live without fear of being separated from our families.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [express donate: $10](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953y62hYNn9HaFt_zHKvI4OP8mx_5ABkFHmeFViL7NEeBD_AogMf18kAB-piHwjkW8FFUOtrwobNpPqIEJougxjxYwA1C7hxAd803SmJBjxIOkFTR_EzQYbNgbrqEc0dR91UxOW4LvXAPRYhjDaDGTx7O1yUzNq2XY6uipVgm39QBVEC4aHSumulJCEAdkate5zAag94rqL5nR7ryGkCjUSdcDjtysPtjBkF2EzQz-fbxQa6Tuen2MB7MU46_XvqMsjG-31dp2s1nw3uHTA8RlWeoY6xjgsHWnRjZNGIg77jAckIbDDgomWwm8EmXFlYtjYmVXqB98yZ4xoP1DdWyFXRM9aUWitH9XDteCQad3Dr9cypDtnFK_J8-odu69_86mpLj7yO6aVPCy8YoWvRi786mR5372EGa5SWMjg0ZRdQcqzxPdWlznJyYmQ8XuFC3TotUkK5VwF964XkAkuxCv3SXdpm8aANcdo7BHBazFp7Sba_KmKizplqBjkqDIqXJPf5XDJK7fAfDd2mmkrubLZQP/3w4/9WFtmZ4AQfW6S7OhW_iDfg/h0/BY56Ddncn5dV6LCBESjN2PiK9uDo_BdQKXvCN-oo02g)

Peter — It’s time for Cameron Sexton to step down.
_First,_ he led the racist charge to expel the two youngest, Black lawmakers in the chamber. While Reps. Jones and Pearson are back where they belong, serving the people of Tennessee, the Speaker has tried to pull every trick in the book to keep them from legislating.
_Then,_ the news dropped that the Speaker spends more of his time in Nashville than his “home” district in Crossville. According to reports, he may have overcharged the people of Tennessee as much as $79,954 since moving to Nashville.
_And it doesn’t stop there,_ it’s now come to light that Speaker Sexton may have had prior knowledge of the sexual harassment incident that led to the resignation of Republican Rep. Scotty Campbell and hid it from the public to cover it up.
Peter — Now, it’s time for Sexton to go. We won’t stand by and let the GOP sweep this repeated corruption under the rug.

First, he led the racist charge to expel the two youngest, Black lawmakers in the chamber. While Reps. Jones and Pearson are back where they belong, serving the people of Tennessee, the Speaker has tried to pull every trick in the book to keep them from legislating. Then, the news dropped that the Speaker spends more of his time in Nashville than his “home” district in Crossville. According to reports, he may have overcharged the people of Tennessee as much as $79,954 since moving to Nashville. And it doesn’t stop there, it’s now come to light that Speaker Sexton may have had prior knowledge of the sexual harassment incident that led to the resignation of Republican Rep. Scotty Campbell and hid it from the public to cover it up. Peter — Now, it’s time for Sexton to go. We won’t stand by and let the GOP sweep this repeated corruption under the rug. Friend,
America is in serious trouble.
Our justice system has been HIJACKED by corrupt political forces who use their power, money, and connections to destroy anyone who stands in their way.
I have once again become the target of a disgusting witch hunt that lacks _ANY_ EVIDENCE, PROOF, or EYEWITNESSES.
**But my freedom and justice did not matter to the Marxists who orchestrated this witch hunt in a city where the voter registration of the jury pool favors Democrats 7:1.**
_This witch hunt was funded by a Democrat mega-donor who slept at Pedophile Jeffrey Epstein 's private island - with a Clinton-appointed judge presiding over the bogus trial._
Let me be as clear as humanly possible: I did NOTHING wrong.
_So, I 'll tell you what this is all about... _
Everybody has seen the polls. No one honestly believes that Joe Biden - sitting at a 36% approval rating - can beat me in a _**fair**_ election next year.
**So, the Democrats have decided that their strategy is to launch witch hunt after witch hunt to try and bleed me dry.**
But the Democrats don't understand that I will NEVER SURRENDER our country to the radical Left. _There is not a single attack on the face of the planet that will break me._
...Because I know that if this left-wing cabal can slander and attack a former U.S. President, they could one day do it to a private citizen like **YOU**.
_I refuse to let that happen. I refuse to let America 's justice system completely collapse._
Together, we will win back the White House and we will SAVE AMERICA.

clean_body: [] It's officially been one month since we launched our campaign, friend! The outpouring of grassroots support has been absolutely incredible, and we wanted to highlight a couple of our biggest moments: We raised $100,000 in the first 72 hours of our race, setting us up with the resources we need to build a winning campaign. Two organizations, League of Conservation Voters and Advancing Arizona, started running ads criticizing my opponent for voting to cut veterans benefits, border security, and more . Last year, our campaign was outspent 6:1, mostly by Kevin McCarthy and GOP dark money groups — so seeing support this early is a great sign. * Our campaign was just endorsed by Moms Fed Up. They join a growing and diverse list of endorsers including Congressman Ruben Gallego, Sheriff Chris Nanos, former Congresswoman Ann Kirkpatrick, and former Congressman Ron Barber. This is a great start — but it will be a long road to 2024. If you want to help our campaign keep up this incredible momentum, here's what you can do: 1. Donate! Whether it's a one-time contribution or a recurring monthly donation, every dollar ensures we won't be outspent in this crucial race! [] Chip in now >> [] 2. Sign our nomination petition! If you live in AZ-06, you can make sure we make it on the ballot by adding your name >> [] Add your name >> [] 3. Follow us on social media! Your support online helps us get our message out and reach new voters. [] [] [] Thanks for all your support! Together, we'll flip this seat blue in November. — Team Kirsten Engel for Arizona [] Kirsten is a mom, an environmental attorney, and a former State Legislator who has spent the past six years fighting to restore funding to public schools, protect Arizona's natural resources, and make our communities safer. She’s ready to take that fight to Washington to find common-sense solutions to the challenges we’re facing here in Arizona's Sixth Congressional District. In 2022, Kirsten lost her race by one of the smallest margins in the country. Will you make sure she can build a winning campaign early by chipping in now? [] Paid for by Engel for Arizona Engel for Arizona PO Box 40721 Tucson, AZ 85717 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please

name: Team AOC
subject: Fewer people donated through email the last couple of weeks than we expected.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
Unlike most other politicians, AOC doesn’t spend her time schmoozing for donations from big donors or at exclusive events. Instead, our campaign relies on online donations from supporters who give $5 or $10 at a time through emails like this one.
But, to be honest, fewer people donated through email the last couple of weeks than we expected.
[Can you chip in right now to fuel our campaign and help us reach our $125,000 mid-month fundraising goal before midnight on the 15th?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230512.aU7KU_&refcode=em20230512-4308-P_FND_MMF_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)

Our campaign takes 0 corporate donations. There’s no super PAC or big donor coming in to protect us from the attacks constantly coming our way. So if our donations drop, as they have been starting to lately, we will have to reassess our budget and make tough cuts. And that’s not where we’d like to be almost halfway through the year.
[A donation of any amount from you today would make a huge impact toward helping us hit our $125,000 fundraising goal before May 15. Can we count on your donation today?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230512.aU7KU_&refcode=em20230512-4308-P_FND_MMF_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230512.aU7KU_&refcode=em20230512-4308-P_FND_MMF_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)**
From all of us here on the campaign, we thank you for your continued support.
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230512?refcode=em20230512-4308-P_FND_MMF_GFM&t=22&refcode2=4308_2526796_vLZErd&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230512-4308-P_FND_MMF_GFM&t=23&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=24&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=25&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=26&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=4308%2E2526796%2EvLZErd)

name: Team Rashida
subject: stand up to attempts to silence Palestinian voices
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
U.S.-funded apartheid | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/ZsYp6Jc2ATNP3MkVzvte6Pmk0F4jS6NOytihmYTOZ1f-yWw6Cj7G9e1dlkX4TL0NMWtZ9DST5cFtdsPS-4D2fKNVbxdzSbLD9T9x2URiTkAkvV0OpQQCoNHFnrXrpjTvDwedyneJzDlNq5SWjZkSu_Ll3r0aTWkt-U2zH8yx3SldYWjR58ESfDcLN9IMS9YkhqZ5jMbroa5aLqg6FSbzL5T2va7i-l0vMB9szYNkWjC-Mm_g27Im7_t0VRt-nu3coLvmOEVGB6dIF7wyduE0mwrPQcrE6sYPwRZkxcsDnEiOaFZKRkjaydEoQrdnpDrEdkdOjopqHFReBlsZKxu_QQ/3w4/iJBS66G_QNG3UHtB3peuJw/h0/fVUnIArYSVVvhushVph6__lobA2eWZbnygy405c2V-Q)
---
Friend, Nine human rights and community organizations planned an event this week to recognize the Nakba on Capitol Hill. Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy tried to silence them and the Palestinian Americans who came to share their stories of survival and pain. The Nakba (Arabic for “catastrophe”) refers to the mass expulsion of Palestinians from their homes and homeland that began 75 years ago during the creation of the state of Israel. In 1948, militias massacred thousands of Indigenous Palestinians and violently displaced three-quarters of the Palestinian population—nearly 800,000 people. The Nakba is a well-documented historical fact and will be recognized by an event at the United Nations next week. **In an attempt to suppress the truth and silence Palestinian voices, McCarthy tried to stop the event by blocking the use of the auditorium it was planned for. But he failed to silence those voices or their truth, as the event proceeded in a different location with a packed, standing-room-only audience.** The Speaker should be focused on the fact that more than 20 veterans organizations have come out opposed to his debt ceiling proposal because it cuts their health benefits, or the fact that one of his Members was just arrested on 13 federal corruption charges. No matter the constant targeting and bullying by extremists in Congress, we must speak out louder than ever about the ongoing injustices here at home and abroad. The U.S. government enables and perpetuates the Israeli government’s war crimes and massacres with U.S.-made weapons and billions in unconditional funding. **This horrific status quo rests on powerful attempts to silence Palestinian voices.** The U.S. Congress and elected officials across the country have even tried to criminalize criticism of Israel’s apartheid regime. But we cannot be silent. We must speak out to recognize the ongoing Nakba and call for Palestinian liberation. [**Please stand up to U.S. and Israeli attempts to silence and erase Palestinians. Chip in what you can to Rashida’s campaign today, as she continues to educate her colleagues and speak up in Congress against apartheid.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wNmphNPncoEzRGQUSC4yLIIAX2ys15gynZqhzKMxz3U2O1xQTin06tfLFCOO2mSpb7535HlxaFDUjxtXN3UnEcqoh3fz8Ui3910gbzn0zfDLdPXnTVQoahiTobvf3Ra9vhZfgFYjndyGpJ8c3I-tmUlrwh9f42cb3itJ-Issv7KOSHqQeMBVqQyhkmf-xFl80ST6atvAK_VLk9dJBKziyM67EfDGfQ4D-vsF92ErXkc8JP_dD_QMGFCByZ9sPUbkBZTsYLen_V87D6Uob-kjxsusxPYKQKvHIwyCoiPO2k6ggvwRUKgzUCwJeBoWFJeJT7KJvTCyucOe-wtjqZweUqKaf4zhXxxL6RJAzdXiI1u6zbeYWU8Mp6R3QEs11-xpWeLPOwGMKiyvwe6z5aK_6o/3w4/iJBS66G_QNG3UHtB3peuJw/h1/pHvg_gwDfjBiJZmE7yseGNtvN0j8BOXVrmTF71SJEuw) | [CHIP IN](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wNmphNPncoEzRGQUSC4yLIIAX2ys15gynZqhzKMxz3U2O1xQTin06tfLFCOO2mSpaB1h2FKhkcSi7A-PZ_mV4cmin6lA6wJRLdZa19soafgKoLawmK8slHBHEfnaNdIqLmZfUJFShOPvIaZXDtMXyCc-qaS_o51ipOVon0ioVrR2Q_SUCvPXm37YSJr53zobkVDc5sdRq4byO9kyIUpzh1rMjCWUM7s_M7e2-bHtJQyoaEcKIvXkZ2qSWElZ0VYdLPMnpMZibXxGCG2pgyeHt5fzqBPH495u4Hlf7Gr5rnzHmNJlQObmlubQmrlm-YapFhwqD-2QTlNrjoV-j-4cJCr4AWNjPZNjrXXgXUGbX69WnCiEPrCW6-Lot1emS0kJpLNrWuBeZ-toXc4efbV4FB/3w4/iJBS66G_QNG3UHtB3peuJw/h2/uNweViiGzMyl16lc5l-MIN-b1sbtunzhzZTsQv37z1E)
---
With Nakba Day coming up, grassroots groups across the U.S. are planning actions, community gatherings, and other commemorations to mark 75 years of the Nakba. [There may be an event near you within the next few days. ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/mRNLFKyANPYkYXuUTzk_SxL-SLMlxlnDr158Oo2oPEwKYkyVj3okPPJC26V3JTBhfyHT9-xEujwOkFRtmjbwu3bpNYEXYBwQ7KmEr5dcd4SpbWZXuhW5NEGRjBKs3X71JmSbkh9YUcfmgTgwQYMd05uUKqkVX_X3XLcFC_UWzEuFrghRvzNQWvX3Gzs6ROGvHkNK1i4cAOgd9ScaSbhAp6qgMbw2ZMGEFlIQEe9NG3eGtzHZp0R-4vUyiWI6FmEZjW--8SSr18rzPx_dtfpWnp0Otk62TBDZ_15EO2YrfFto-GnClqvnINVUFr6xSXfSYSdUO4hZLl7VCb_sglgG-A/3w4/iJBS66G_QNG3UHtB3peuJw/h3/VOS8Ca_so0FYL1gZZ1JF0f5TpzKkvOfEgtpq0bC8CZI)

Team Rashida

The U.S. Congress and elected officials across the country have even tried to criminalize criticism of Israel’s apartheid regime. But we cannot be silent. We must speak out to recognize the ongoing Nakba and call for Palestinian liberation. | With Nakba Day coming up, grassroots groups across the U.S. are planning actions, community gatherings, and other commemorations to mark 75 years of the Nakba. In solidarity, Team Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . body:
Democrats in their attempt to sabotage our elections rallied millions of their supporters in favor of ERASING Voter ID laws across the country.

| |
---
| | | It's hard to express how utterly humiliated we feel at this moment.

Democrats in their attempt to sabotage our elections rallied millions of their supporters in favor of ERASING Voter ID laws across the country.

_**In response, Republicans started our own petition to protect these laws and ensure every election in the country is safe and secure.**_

But the number of responses we got was embarrassing... and the defeat we may face in the next election because of it will be even more heartbreaking.

But we've got another chance, Friend. This fight is too crucial and our election integrity is too important to quit. **[So we're asking ONE FINAL TIME: will you sign the petition and help us secure our elections right here and now?](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=adeeb76985c34122947b4b656278236e&_e=FBvdl-Dtehgw20D1XBlRNlUXSLHmNyx__dPjsjExBdLNGjeTDHVMNuUWjYBCCc5ZzkvUo5-ylzv9BQpGYibNLRX546_fdrlgNnqP7nRcwPQJ1rNmifafaPCsDrClUO9WOGZEozPsUN9Ybh14%3D "So we’re asking ONE FINAL TIME: will you sign the petition and help us secure our elections right here and now?")**

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: June 1
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
The Republicans in Congress need to get their act together and prevent a national debt default. | | | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63219183/405986613/-1964596891?ms=em-header&refcode=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzgxOGQxNy0xNmYxLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGzfUcZv9stvFqJF_PKzbt5CGS1M3fVa6sfL5-L7p0o=&emci=d6818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=d7818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9027908)
---
“X-Date” sounds like something out of a fantasy action movie — but it’s real, and it’s coming up fast. That’s the term people are using to describe the TBD date when the United States government won’t be able to pay its bills anymore — unless Congress takes action and raises the debt ceiling. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen recently announced that the “X-Date” could come as early as June 1, which is less than three weeks away. The consequences of a debt default would be devastating. Millions of jobs lost. Our economy plunged into a recession. Countries around the world seeing the United States not live up to its word. Democrats are ready to prevent that nightmare scenario. We just need the Republicans to step up, do their jobs already, and stop trying to extort us into making devastating budget cuts. Now, I used to be a teacher, so I’m used to seeing students rush to get things done at the last minute — not that I ever called a deadline an “X-Date” on a syllabus. But there’s no reason for Congress to wait — and force Americans to twiddle their thumbs and wonder if we’re going to default or not. There’s no reason to let any doubt linger on the world stage about our nation’s ability to honor our obligations. And there’s no no reason why GOP House Speaker Kevin McCarthy can’t immediately pass a clean bill to avoid a debt default — like the THREE debt ceiling increases he voted for when Donald Trump was president. [_**Add your name if you agree: Republicans must get serious and stop playing negotiation games while Americans’ livelihoods hang in the balance.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63219184/405986614/1341464772?refcode=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&ms=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzgxOGQxNy0xNmYxLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGzfUcZv9stvFqJF_PKzbt5CGS1M3fVa6sfL5-L7p0o=&emci=d6818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=d7818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9027908) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63219185/405986615/1341464772?refcode=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&ms=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzgxOGQxNy0xNmYxLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGzfUcZv9stvFqJF_PKzbt5CGS1M3fVa6sfL5-L7p0o=&emci=d6818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=d7818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9027908)
---
Now, look: I was saying last year that we needed to raise the debt ceiling before the Republican wrecking-ball crew took over the House. In fact, I suggested we should _eliminate_ the debt ceiling altogether, because it serves no purpose — Congress already signs off on spending through an entirely separate process.
But here we are. And we’ve got to navigate these choppy waters as best we can.
Congressional Republicans are holding our economy hostage. They’re trying to use the debt ceiling as negotiating leverage. And you know what they want in exchange for not tanking the economy? Slashing programs like nutrition assistance, and rental assistance, and Meals on Wheels. Threatening veterans’ health care. Unwinding President Biden’s student debt cancelation plan.
Look: if they want to talk about budget plans, we can have that conversation when it’s time to sign off on spending. But we can’t negotiate over whether the United States pays its bills on spending that Congress has _already approved_.
I might add that the GOP blew up the deficit by handing massive tax breaks to billionaires and giant corporations — so if they really want to reduce the debt, they can by rolling those back.
[_**I’m staying in the fight to prevent a debt default and stop Republicans from making devastating budget cuts. I’m asking you to add your name, raise your voice, and say you’re in this fight, too. Together, let’s hold the line.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63219186/405986616/1341464772?refcode=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&ms=EMZ13S_FIN_20230512_V1S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzgxOGQxNy0xNmYxLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGzfUcZv9stvFqJF_PKzbt5CGS1M3fVa6sfL5-L7p0o=&emci=d6818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=d7818d17-16f1-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=9027908)
Thanks for being a part of this,
Elizabeth

clean_body: The Republicans in Congress need to get their act together and prevent a national debt default. | | | “X-Date” sounds like something out of a fantasy action movie — but it’s real, and it’s coming up fast. That’s the term people are using to describe the TBD date when the United States government won’t be able to pay its bills anymore — unless Congress takes action and raises the debt ceiling. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen recently announced that the “X-Date” could come as early as June 1, which is less than three weeks away. The consequences of a debt default would be devastating. Millions of jobs lost. Our economy plunged into a recession. Countries around the world seeing the United States not live up to its word. Democrats are ready to prevent that nightmare scenario. We just need the Republicans to step up, do their jobs already, and stop trying to extort us into making devastating budget cuts. Now, I used to be a teacher, so I’m used to seeing students rush to get things done at the last minute — not that I ever called a deadline an “X-Date” on a syllabus. But there’s no reason for Congress to wait — and force Americans to twiddle their thumbs and wonder if we’re going to default or not. There’s no reason to let any doubt linger on the world stage about our nation’s ability to honor our obligations. And there’s no no reason why GOP House Speaker Kevin McCarthy can’t immediately pass a clean bill to avoid a debt default — like the THREE debt ceiling increases he voted for when Donald Trump was president. | Now, look: I was saying last year that we needed to raise the debt ceiling before the Republican wrecking-ball crew took over the House. In fact, I suggested we should eliminate the debt ceiling altogether, because it serves no purpose — Congress already signs off on spending through an entirely separate process. But here we are. And we’ve got to navigate these choppy waters as best we can. Congressional Republicans are holding our economy hostage. They’re trying to use the debt ceiling as negotiating leverage. And you know what they want in exchange for not tanking the economy? Slashing programs like nutrition assistance, and rental assistance, and Meals on Wheels. Threatening veterans’ health care. Unwinding President Biden’s student debt cancelation plan. Look: if they want to talk about budget plans, we can have that conversation when it’s time to sign off on spending. But we can’t negotiate over whether the United States pays its bills on spending that Congress has already approved. I might add that the GOP blew up the deficit by handing massive tax breaks to billionaires and giant corporations — so if they really want to reduce the debt, they can by rolling those back. Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth = | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for Senate All content © 2023 Warren for Senate, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: The House just passed a dangerous resolution to increase support for Israel’s occupation.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

The U.S. House of Representatives recently passed a resolution to
encourage deepening support for Israel and expanding the Abraham Accords,
Trump-backed agreements between Israel and Arab countries that ignore
Palestinians and enable human rights abuses in the Middle East and Africa.

Members of Congress who voted for the resolution noted that their votes
were in honor of the 75th anniversary of Israel’s founding as a state. But
they ignore the reality of how the state of Israel was founded 75 years
ago: by killing and displacing two-thirds of the Indigenous Palestinian
population, in a brutal campaign that Palestinians call the “Nakba,” or
catastrophe.

We have to end U.S. military aid to Israel’s apartheid regime, not double
down on uncritical support for Israel’s human rights abuses.

U.S. military funding supports the Israeli government’s war crimes, to the
tune of billions of U.S. taxpayer dollars each year. In Congress, the
bipartisan support for unconditional funding of Israel’s military shows
how far we still have to go in the struggle for Palestinian freedom.
However, 18 Democrats stood up for Palestinian rights by opposing the
recent House resolution—an increase that reflects a huge shift in U.S.
public opinion in favor of Palestinian rights.

As the only Palestinian-American in Congress, I know my advocacy in
Congress disrupts the unjust status quo. I will continue to speak up until
we achieve safety and justice for all.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134440?refcode=em20230512-28918&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]Will you split a $3 donation between myself and Justice
Democrats to support our work to grow the U.S. movement for Palestinian
liberation?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134440?refcode=em20230512-28918&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]Contribute $3

The passage of House Resolution 311 shows just how out of step Congress is
with the ongoing shift in American public opinion towards support for
Palestinians and against the Israeli government’s extremism. Unlike prior
versions of this resolution, this Republican-drafted version fails to even
mention Palestinians or attempts at peace.

Instead, the resolution encourages expanding the Abraham Accords, which
were pushed by the Trump administration and signed in 2020 to establish
ties between Israel and Arab countries. These deals further enable
Israel’s impunity and U.S. weapons sales while undermining human rights.
One leader in the negotiations that led to the Accords even admitted that
the intent was to marginalize Palestinians.^1

As the top supporter of the Israeli military, the U.S. has the leverage
needed to demand equal rights for Palestinians. It's our responsibility to
keep pushing for change. U.S. taxpayer dollars fund Israel’s apartheid
regime, which restricts basic resources and freedoms from Palestinians, in
an ongoing attempt to erase an entire people.

We must urgently grow our movement to end U.S. complicity in Israel’s
ethnic cleansing, military occupation, and apartheid. And we need your
support to do so.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134440?refcode=em20230512-28918&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]Please contribute what you can today to Justice Democrats and my
campaign so we can keep fighting for Palestinian rights.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134440?refcode=em20230512-28918&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1040&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]Contribute $3

Thank you.

In solidarity,

Rashida

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

1 - [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134448?t=1041&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]imeu.org

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1042&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1043&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6 ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230512-28918-footer&t=1044&refcode2=28918_1203867_cIwu_6&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1045&akid=28918%2E1203867%2EcIwu_6

Those in attendance included**several folks who volunteered for Sinema when she was running for Senate in 2018** –and they had quite a few grievances to air. They’ve tried time and again to get in touch with Sinema with no success–and they’re fed up with being put second to billionaires, Wall Street executives, and Sinema’s masseuse.
When Sinema was elected in 2018, she had a choice to make: use her influence and power to support Arizona families, or sell out. Sinema chose limos, private jets, fancy dinners, and expensive wine. **Sinema chose to sell out.**

name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: This Mother’s Day
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
I am grateful that I’ll get to spend this Mother’s Day with my own fabulous mom. And I look forward to celebrating the many wonderful mothers who lead us with strength, vision, and clarity.
We are lucky to have so many moms across the country working so hard in so many ways for our communities and country. And we have to work for them. Every day.
We know that in Texas and across the country, moms face many challenges—from ensuring access to affordable child care to securing equal pay for equal work to surviving pregnancy itself.
It may seem hard to believe, but it is true: Our country has the highest maternal mortality rate in the developed world. Since the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade and states enacted even more restrictions to access to critical reproductive health care, experts predict that the maternal mortality rate will rise even more. We also know that many of the conditions that have led to this high death rate are preventable.
That is why I am working in Congress to address these issues and disparities. As whip of the Pro-Choice Caucus and a founding member of the Black Maternal Health Caucus, I’ve made it a priority to find policy solutions for the issues facing mothers, especially around reproductive health care. From securing funding for programs that support Black maternal health in Harris County to introducing and voting for legislation that ensures women’s rights to make our own reproductive health care decisions, I am working with a broad coalition to give moms the support they need.
The work we do together is a critical part of that effort. By working together to elect people who will prioritize the health, dignity, and freedom of women across the country, who will work in partnership to solve real problems and protect our rights, we are making a real difference. I am grateful to everyone who has made it possible for me to do this work, and I am committed to continuing it.
It is with that in mind, that I send wishes for a happy Mother’s Day to all the moms of #TeamLizzie!
Together, we can do anything,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

Peter, we have some exciting news!

_Lori has been working hard to improve the I-5 Bridge Project over the Columbia River. This project is crucial for our region's transportation infrastructure and economic growth.
_
But as we all know, large infrastructure projects require significant resources to become a reality. We need to make sure Lori has the resources she needs to continue fighting for this project.

clean_body: | | | | Lori Does! Peter, we have some exciting news! Lori has been working hard to improve the I-5 Bridge Project over the Columbia River. This project is crucial for our region's transportation infrastructure and economic growth. But as we all know, large infrastructure projects require significant resources to become a reality. We need to make sure Lori has the resources she needs to continue fighting for this project. | With your help, we can make sure Lori is successful in her efforts to secure the funding needed to get this project off the ground. Any amount, no matter how small, will help us reach our goal. Together, we can make sure the I-5 Bridge Project becomes a reality. Thank you, Team Lori | | | | | | | | | Even as a Congresswoman, Lori’s most important title is mom. That’s how she knows the issues your family faces, and that’s why she fights everyday to make life better for every Oregon family, and every American. Aside from that, all you need to know are the issues she’s fighting for in Congress: If that sounds like the kind of agenda you can get behind, If you’d prefer to give by check, please use the below address: Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Congress 13203 SE 172nd Ave Suite 166 #399 Happy Valley, OR 97086 Forward This message to FIVE FRIENDS AND FAMILY to spread the word on how to achieve a better future for Oregon and America → ** Also text “LORI” to 71800 or click here to receive all of Lori’s messages | | | | Team Lori 13203 SE 172nd Ave, Suite 166 #399, Happy Valley, OR 97086 \- =

name: Matt Barr
subject: To my favorite military spouse:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Callie Barr for Congress
body:
I want to take a moment to say THANK YOU to all the military spouses out there - those who served and are still serving. | Peter, It's Military Spouse Appreciation Day, so I want to take a moment to say THANK YOU to all the military spouses out there – those who served and are still serving. I also want to talk about my favorite military spouse: my wife Callie. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63204862/405953236/-621152178?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230512milspouse_mb_full_xx_img1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2YwMmNmOGEtZjdmMC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sQ3bjNdXOrsHyLVHxsGM524-3VSS-f8CHtpPJziZUn4=)
---
From the moment I made the call to the Marine Corps recruiter on September 11 until the time I retired, **I relied on Callie’s grit and strength, which has always been a constant in our lives.**
She shouldered the stress and fear of my two deployments to Iraq, including my first deployment to Fallujah where many of our friends were killed or injured. Callie sent care packages, Bible verses, anything to give me hope that I would make it home. At times, I wasn’t certain I would.
Then, there was the challenge of my injuries: an undiagnosed traumatic brain injury and PTS. Callie spent her time advocating for me and military families like ours to get us the help we needed.
Did I mention she did all this while going back to school _and_ moving 5 times in 15 years _and_ raising our two daughters?
**Callie could have chosen to accept the status quo and accept the hand we were dealt – but that's not who she is.** She met other military spouses going through the exact same struggles with accessing care, and so she made it her mission to give a voice to those who made the greatest sacrifice. Then she went to law school and used her degree to provide free legal services for veterans.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63204863/405953237/-621152178?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230512milspouse_mb_full_xx_img2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2YwMmNmOGEtZjdmMC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sQ3bjNdXOrsHyLVHxsGM524-3VSS-f8CHtpPJziZUn4=)
---
We talk a lot about the commitment and dedication of military service members. **But it also takes a lot of patience and courage to be a military spouse, too.**
It's why I know Callie will be an amazing advocate for the families like ours of Northern Michigan and the Upper Peninsula. She doesn’t know how to give up. And **there's nothing Congress could throw at her that she can't handle.**
Thanks for reading – thanks to all military spouses – and thank you for supporting Callie,
Matt Barr

| Callie Barr is a 5th-generation Michigander, advocate for veterans, and lawyer. She’s running to take on extremism and do-nothing politicians in D.C. that are leaving rural America behind. Can you help send Callie to Congress by donating today?
---
| [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63204864/405953238/-621152178?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230512milspouse_mb_full_xx_ftr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2YwMmNmOGEtZjdmMC1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sQ3bjNdXOrsHyLVHxsGM524-3VSS-f8CHtpPJziZUn4=)
---

Friends,
This is a quick but important note as we head into the weekend. Sara and the rest of the team are really grateful for this space - for the chance to talk with you about the issues that matter most, and for the opportunity to hear your perspectives and your stories.
There are also times, like around the upcoming Mother’s Day holiday, when these issues can be especially difficult for some, and we want to honor those needs too. If you would like to skip the email Sara will be sending this weekend about Mother’s Day, click the link below to opt out of that content.
I'LL OPT OUT [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Kv-KPfW_uECGJVB1xhwjQA2]
Together, we can tackle some of our biggest challenges if we rely on each other for strength and support. And sometimes that means taking a beat to preserve our energy and our mental health. We hope you’ll take that space if you need it.
Thanks for being here.
-Team Sara
